name: io.github.aliasunder/vault-cortex
title: Vault Cortex
description: Standalone MCP server for Obsidian vaults — hybrid search, notes, memory, tasks, OAuth 2.1.

runtimeArguments.name: -p
runtimeArguments.description: Publish the container's port 8000 on the host.

runtimeArguments.name: -v
runtimeArguments.description: Bind-mount your Obsidian vault into the container at /vault.

VAULT_PATH.description: Absolute path to your Obsidian vault on the host machine.

runtimeArguments.name: -v
runtimeArguments.description: Named volume for persistent state under /data — search index, OAuth token DB, and any log files. Keeps OAuth sessions alive across container restarts.

headers.name: Authorization
headers.description: Bearer token used by the MCP client. Must match the MCP_AUTH_TOKEN env var passed to the container.

MCP_AUTH_TOKEN.description: Bearer token for MCP client authentication. Generate with: openssl rand -hex 32

environmentVariables.name: MCP_AUTH_TOKEN
environmentVariables.description: Bearer token for MCP client authentication. Must match the Authorization header sent by clients. Generate with: openssl rand -hex 32

environmentVariables.name: PUBLIC_URL
environmentVariables.description: Public URL clients use to reach this server. Used as the OAuth issuer URL in discovery metadata. Override when exposing the server outside localhost or on a non-default port.

environmentVariables.name: EMBEDDING_ENABLED
environmentVariables.description: Enable or disable the embedding pipeline. When false, no ONNX model is downloaded, no vector tables are created, and search uses FTS5 only.

environmentVariables.name: RERANK_MODE
environmentVariables.description: Cross-encoder reranking mode: blended (position-aware score blending after RRF fusion) or none (skip reranking). Only takes effect when EMBEDDING_ENABLED is true.

environmentVariables.name: WINDOWS_MODE
environmentVariables.description: Windows bind-mount mode: enables filesystem polling for the file watcher and rename-based moves across the Docker Desktop/WSL2 bridge. Set to true when the vault lives on a Windows drive.

environmentVariables.name: MEMORY_ENABLED
environmentVariables.description: Enable or disable the structured memory layer. When false, memory tools are hidden, bootstrap is skipped, and server metadata omits memory references.

environmentVariables.name: MEMORY_DIR
environmentVariables.description: Vault folder for structured memory files (About Me-style notes). Memory tools are hidden when MEMORY_ENABLED is false, but this value still feeds the defaults for PROTECTED_PATHS and ORPHAN_EXCLUDE_FOLDERS.

environmentVariables.name: TZ
environmentVariables.description: IANA timezone for timestamps and daily note resolution.

environmentVariables.name: LOG_DIR
environmentVariables.description: Directory for persistent log files. Unset by default — logs go to stdout only. Set to /data/logs to also write date-stamped .log files to the persistent volume.

environmentVariables.name: LOG_RETENTION_DAYS
environmentVariables.description: Days to retain persistent log files before cleanup.

environmentVariables.name: PROTECTED_PATHS
environmentVariables.description: Comma-separated vault folder names blocked from vault_delete_note. Default: MEMORY_DIR and "Daily Notes".

environmentVariables.name: ORPHAN_EXCLUDE_FOLDERS
environmentVariables.description: Comma-separated vault folder names excluded from vault_find_orphans. Default: "Daily Notes", "Templates", MEMORY_DIR.

environmentVariables.name: SERVICE_DOCUMENTATION_URL
environmentVariables.description: Override the OAuth service documentation URL exposed via discovery metadata.

environmentVariables.name: MAX_ASSET_BYTES
environmentVariables.description: Largest asset file vault_read_asset will read, in bytes. Reading a larger file returns an error instead of content.

environmentVariables.name: MAX_IMAGE_OUTPUT_BYTES
environmentVariables.description: Byte budget for images returned by vault_read_asset, in binary bytes before base64 encoding. Images exceeding the budget are downscaled/recompressed server-side to fit; raise for clients that accept larger tool responses.
